title: Robotics Perception Starter
description: Train and serve a perception model that can become the first portfolio signal for autonomy, robotics, or edge-inference roles.
whyItMatters: This is the optional bridge into the robotics/autonomy specialization track, where perception and hardware constraints matter.

steps.title: Choose a dataset
steps.description: Download a classification dataset from Kaggle or torchvision. Good options: CIFAR-10 (10 classes, good for learning), Food-101 (real-world, harder), or create your own by scraping images.
steps.tips: Start with a small dataset (1000-5000 images). You can always scale up once your pipeline works.

steps.title: Set up data loading
steps.description: Use PyTorch DataLoaders with torchvision transforms. Implement data augmentation: random crops, horizontal flips, colour jitter, rotation. Split into train/validation/test sets.

steps.title: Load a pre-trained model
steps.description: Use torchvision to load a pre-trained ResNet-50 or EfficientNet-B0. Replace the final classification layer to match your number of classes. Freeze the early layers initially.

steps.title: Train the model
steps.description: Write the training loop: forward pass, compute loss (CrossEntropyLoss), backward pass, optimiser step (Adam). Track training and validation loss/accuracy each epoch. Train for 10-20 epochs.

steps.title: Visualise training
steps.description: Plot training vs validation loss curves. Are they converging? Is there a gap (overfitting)? Try unfreezing more layers and reducing learning rate if the model plateaus.

steps.title: Evaluate thoroughly
steps.description: Generate a classification report and confusion matrix on the test set. Which classes does the model confuse? Look at misclassified examples — do they make sense?

steps.title: Deploy as an API
steps.description: Save the model and create a FastAPI endpoint that accepts an image upload and returns the predicted class with confidence scores.

deliverables: Training curves showing loss and accuracy
Brief write-up of experiments and findings

rubric.description: The model achieves at least 85% accuracy on the test set (for standard datasets like CIFAR-10).
Training curves show proper convergence. Evidence of addressing overfitting through augmentation or regularisation.
Confusion matrix analysis identifies which classes are confused and why. Misclassified examples are examined.
Training loop is clean and modular. Data loading, model definition, training, and evaluation are in separate functions or files.
